name: MITRE.LIN.T1052.RULE Exfiltration Over Physical Medium
notes:
# CAN BE NOISY! Add additional tests to narrow down the scope.
		
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

auditctl -a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S mount -S umount2 -k siem-mount

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-mount
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1052.RULE Exfiltration Over Physical Medium on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-mount
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1052.RULE Exfiltration Over Physical Medium

Event description: In certain circumstances, such as an air-gapped network compromise, exfiltration could occur via a physical medium or device introduced by a user. Such media could be an external hard drive, USB drive, cellular phone, MP3 player, or other removable storage and processing device.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1052/
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
